claim: PPAR-RXRs can be activated by PPAR ligands.

result.claim: PPAR-RXRs can be activated by PPAR ligands.
result.reasoning: The searches conducted did not yield direct evidence to support or refute the claim that PPAR-RXRs can be activated by PPAR ligands. The results primarily discussed RXR's role as a heterodimerization partner for other nuclear receptors (RAR, T3R) and general concepts of ligand-dependent conformational changes in nuclear receptors. There was no specific mention of PPAR ligands activating the PPAR-RXR heterodimer. Therefore, there is insufficient evidence in the knowledge base to verify this claim.

evidence.text: VgrG-PAAR spike complex is decorated with multiple effectors.
evidence.source: PAAR-repeat proteins sharpen and diversify the Type VI secretion system spike

evidence.text: PAAR proteins are further involved in attaching effector domains to the spike.
evidence.source: PAAR-repeat proteins sharpen and diversify the Type VI secretion system spike

evidence.text: RXR is active as a non-DNA-binding cofactor with the NGFI-B/Nurr1 orphan receptors.
evidence.source: Unique response pathways are established by allosteric interactions among nuclear hormone receptors

evidence.text: Proteins from the PAAR (proline-alanine-alanine-arginine) repeat superfamily form a sharp conical extension on the VgrG spike.
evidence.source: PAAR-repeat proteins sharpen and diversify the Type VI secretion system spike

evidence.text: Activation of class B G protein-coupled receptors is driven by interaction of the peptide N terminus with the receptor core.
evidence.source: The Extracellular Surface of the GLP-1 Receptor Is a Molecular Trigger for Biased Agonism

evidence.text: The crystal structures of two PAAR-repeat proteins bound to VgrG-like partners show that these proteins sharpen the tip of the T6SS spike complex.
evidence.source: PAAR-repeat proteins sharpen and diversify the Type VI secretion system spike

evidence.text: T cell receptor (TCR-CD3) triggering involves both receptor clustering and conformational changes at the cytoplasmic tails of the CD3 subunits.
evidence.source: Full activation of the T cell receptor requires both clustering and conformational changes at CD3.

evidence.text: The 9-cis retinoic acid receptor (RXR) serves as a common heterodimerization partner for the thyroid hormone receptor (T3R).
evidence.source: Unique response pathways are established by allosteric interactions among nuclear hormone receptors

evidence.text: RLRs cooperate in signaling crosstalk networks with Toll-like receptors.
The VgrG-PAAR spike complex is decorated with multiple effectors that are delivered simultaneously into target cells.
evidence.source: PAAR-repeat proteins sharpen and diversify the Type VI secretion system spike

evidence.text: Nuclear receptors undergo ligand-dependent conformational changes that are required for corepressor-coactivator exchange.
evidence.source: Histone Methylation-Dependent Mechanisms Impose Ligand Dependency for Gene Activation by Nuclear Receptors

evidence.text: Mutant Ras was a determinant of resistance to PX-866.
evidence.source: Mutations in the phosphatidylinositol-3-kinase pathway predict for antitumor activity of the inhibitor PX-866 whereas oncogenic Ras is a dominant predictor for resistance.

evidence.text: The 9-cis retinoic acid receptor (RXR) serves as a common heterodimerization partner for the retinoic acid receptor (RAR).
evidence.source: Unique response pathways are established by allosteric interactions among nuclear hormone receptors
